meaning: adj.(来源,资料等)直接的,直接得来的,直接采购的adv.第一手地,直接地
examples: School trips give children firsthand experience not available in the classroom.
She sailed for Europe as an accredited correspondent for Life for a firsthand look at the war.
Michael Zhao has seen the damage firsthand.
But when IT and medical products crashed in 2000, Firsthand began losing money fast.
In the mid-1990s Kam and a partner started a mutual fund called Firsthand Funds.
